raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "reporting an unexpected observational change following an unspecified action",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "an action by 'he' precedes and triggers the change",
    "measurable or noticeable improvement occurred in respiration",
    "measurable or noticeable improvement occurred in walking",
    "the degree of improvement qualifies as 'remarkable'",
    "the observer experienced genuine astonishment",
    "the observation is reliable and noteworthy enough to report"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent as a first-person observational report; no logical contradictions, though causal and evaluative terms ('remarkable', astonishment) rest on subjective judgment",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the referent of 'he' and the action 'does so' must be identifiable; the observation must be accurate, replicable, or clinically relevant; context must clarify the underlying condition being treated",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the reported improvement is fabricated, exaggerated, or placebo-driven; the causal link to the action is spurious; key context (e.g., patient identity, medical condition, prior state) is omitted or distorted"
}
